title: Agent Chat Onboarding — Auto-join #general on Startup
description:
When agents start up, they go straight to claiming tasks without joining chat channels or announcing themselves. The human watching the chat panel sees zero activity — agents are invisible.

Root causes:
1. Role docs (workflow step 1) say "Claim task" — no mention of joining #general first
2. Agent scripts (moe-agent.sh/.ps1) PROMPT says "call moe.claim_next_task" — no chat step
3. claimNextTask posts a system message to the task channel but never touches #general
4. No auto-join exists anywhere in the codebase — joining is entirely manual

Fix with two layers (belt & suspenders):
1. Code: claimNextTask auto-announces new workers to #general (guaranteed, can't be ignored)
2. Docs: Role docs + agent scripts updated to include chat join as step 1 (agents also self-announce)
definitionOfDone: claimNextTask auto-posts to #general when a new worker is registered
claimNextTask auto-sets chatCursors for the task channel on the worker
docs/agent-context.md has a Startup section with chat join instructions
All 3 role docs (architect/worker/qa) have 'Join #general' as workflow step 1
initFiles.ts embedded role docs match the external doc changes
Agent scripts (moe-agent.sh and moe-agent.ps1) PROMPT and SHORT_PROMPT include chat join step
StateManager tests cover new claimNextTask behavior
npm run build succeeds with no type errors
npm test passes with no regressions

implementationPlan.note: Added auto-announce to #general on new worker creation, auto-set chatCursors for task channel, and generalChannelId in response. All additions wrapped in try/catch to never block the core claim operation.

implementationPlan.description:
Update docs/agent-context.md — Add Startup section.

Before "## Chat Communication" (line 38), insert:
```markdown
## Startup (Do This First)

Before claiming tasks, announce yourself in #general:
1. `moe.chat_channels` — find the channel with `type: "general"`
2. `moe.chat_join { channel: "<id>", workerId: "<your-id>" }`
3. `moe.chat_send { channel: "<id>", workerId: "<your-id>", content: "Online as <role>. Ready to work." }`
```

Update "## Chat Communication" intro to:
```markdown
The project has a `#general` channel for cross-role announcements. Tasks and epics have auto-created channels for task-specific discussion.
```
implementationPlan.note: Added Startup section before Chat Communication with 3-step chat join instructions. Updated Chat Communication intro to mention #general channel.

Renumber remaining steps (2-6).

**docs/roles/worker.md** (line 7): Same — prepend step 1, renumber rest (2-7).

**docs/roles/qa.md** (line 7): Same — prepend step 1, renumber rest (2-5).
implementationPlan.note: Added 'Join #general' as workflow step 1 in all 3 role docs. Renumbered subsequent steps accordingly.

implementationPlan.description:
Update agent script PROMPT and SHORT_PROMPT in moe-agent.sh.

1. **PROMPT (line 1084)**: Prepend chat join instruction before the existing text:
`"First call moe.chat_channels to find #general, then moe.chat_join and moe.chat_send to announce yourself as $ROLE. Then call moe.get_pending_questions..."`

2. **SHORT_PROMPT for Codex (lines 1121, 1123)**: Prepend 'join #general via moe.chat_join + moe.chat_send' before 'call moe.claim_next_task'.

3. **SHORT_PROMPT for Gemini (lines 1166, 1168)**: Same changes.
implementationPlan.note: Updated PROMPT with chat join prefix. Updated all 4 SHORT_PROMPT variants (Codex auto/manual, Gemini auto/manual) to include 'join #general' step before claim_next_task.

implementationPlan.description:
Add test coverage for new claimNextTask chat behavior.

In `packages/moe-daemon/src/state/StateManager.test.ts`, add a `describe('claimNextTask chat integration')` block:
1. Test: posts to #general on new worker creation (spy on postToGeneral, verify called with workerId + role)
2. Test: does NOT post to #general for existing worker (create worker first, verify postToGeneral not called again)
3. Test: sets chatCursors for task channel (verify worker.chatCursors includes task channel ID)
4. Test: response includes generalChannelId (create general channel, verify field in response)
5. Test: generalChannelId is null when no general channel exists

Import claimNextTaskTool and call handler directly using existing test helper patterns. Error handling: each test should verify the tool returns successfully even if chat operations fail.
implementationPlan.note: Added 5 tests in 'claimNextTask chat integration' describe block covering: #general announcement on new worker, no duplicate announcement for existing worker, chatCursors auto-set, generalChannelId present/null in response.

implementationPlan.description:
Build and run test suite to verify everything compiles and passes.

Run `npx tsc --noEmit` and `npx vitest run` in packages/moe-daemon. Fix any compilation or test failures. Verify no regressions in existing tests.
implementationPlan.note: tsc --noEmit passes cleanly. All 303 tests pass across 16 test files. Fixed test helpers to use migration-created channels instead of creating duplicates. No regressions.
